[2018-03-27 20:58] Deleted User (Deleted User): You buy arms race when inflation is low, ie when the opp gets value of the engineers much later than ~2 turns from now
[2018-03-27 20:58] http404error: The free value pays off well later but you could lose that in a breach or something because of the value you gave by losing attack at a critical moment
[2018-03-27 20:58] Deleted User (Deleted User): if you've bought the first arms race, the next few self-synergise because the engineer dump makes it lower inflation and you've alr got the tech
[2018-03-27 20:58] Deleted User (Deleted User): but still, you rarely buy the 4th(according to punf)
[2018-03-27 20:58] Deleted User (Deleted User): yeah frontline is a thing... L
[2018-03-27 20:59] Deleted User (Deleted User): Punf logic actually is arms race with 3 supply is a buff 😄
[2018-03-27 20:59] http404error: @Ponnari The set of things that are included in SDR is pretty well-defined and not really up for personal interpretation. It definitely doesn't include golden shield.
[2018-03-27 21:01] ponnari: @http404error afaik it is not well defined
[2018-03-27 21:01] http404error: The confusion stems from the article, which doesn't strictly define it.
[2018-03-27 21:01] ponnari: like if we look at the orginal article, it lays out one dominance realation then states general statements about the others
[2018-03-27 21:02] ponnari: are they defined somewhere public?
[2018-03-27 21:02] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): So less defined and more consensus
[2018-03-27 21:02] http404error: There are definitely some hard conclusions we can make based on the info
[2018-03-27 21:02] Deleted User (Deleted User): I actually agree with the punf logic. I'd rather have 4 supply arms race, but it's highly likely the 4 supply arms race has a statistically lower wr than the 3 sup one
[2018-03-27 21:02] http404error: There are fuzzy areas to my understanding
[2018-03-27 21:03] http404error: But they also don't matter
[2018-03-27 21:03] Deleted User (Deleted User): How valuable is statistical wrs to calculating how strong a unit is?
[2018-03-27 21:03] http404error: SDR is more limited than people seem to think
[2018-03-27 21:03] http404error: dominance relations all follow the same form given, it's just the values that change
[2018-03-27 21:04] http404error: the mistake people often make is using SDR to extrapolate about hypothetical balance patches (like "does SS dominate non-Blocker SS"?) when really those questions are not so important
[2018-03-27 21:05] Deleted User (Deleted User): why is it not so important?
[2018-03-27 21:06] http404error: because non-Blocker SS doesn't exist in the game
[2018-03-27 21:06] Deleted User (Deleted User): I guess it's cheaper
[2018-03-27 21:07] http404error: well that's obviously a tradeoff, which SDR doesn't apply to
[2018-03-27 21:07] ponnari: @http404error  Usually when we say an ability breaks SDR, we mean that there is no dominance relation to having vs not having that ability. If we think in this way then it's easy to show that golden armor breaks SDR, yet you claim it does not. May I ask you to elaborate?
[2018-03-27 21:07] http404error: even Drone vs Vivid Drone is a tradeoff
[2018-03-27 21:07] http404error: because Drone can be used to make Forcefields, etc
[2018-03-27 21:07] http404error: @Ponnari Yes, you may ask
[2018-03-27 21:07] http404error: Lots of things in the game do not dominate
[2018-03-27 21:08] http404error: Take the example I just said
[2018-03-27 21:08] http404error: Does Vivid Drone break SDR?
[2018-03-27 21:09] http404error: Just to be clear, let's say we consider the hypothetical situation of having a Vivid Drone versus a Drone
[2018-03-27 21:09] http404error: Neither dominates the other, obviously
[2018-03-27 21:10] http404error: But that fact doesn't mean it breaks SDR
[2018-03-27 21:10] http404error: It's just... part of the game
[2018-03-27 21:11] http404error: Sorry if that seems inane, I'm going super slow with the argument.
[2018-03-27 21:11] ponnari: I'd say that comparing viivd to drone is not a comparison of single abtributes
[2018-03-27 21:11] http404error: Right, getting there
[2018-03-27 21:11] http404error: Now consider the case in the article, where you have a Lucina and a Drone versus Lucina and a Golden Armor Drone.
[2018-03-27 21:11] ponnari: like we can clearly say that tarsier dominates hell hound
[2018-03-27 21:12] http404error: If you'll pardon me for running with that aside for a moment
[2018-03-27 21:12] http404error: A hypothetical unit that liked hellhounds or disliked tarsiers would change that dominance relationship
[2018-03-27 21:12] http404error: Such a unit would not break SDR
[2018-03-27 21:12] http404error: not all dominance relations in the game fall under the umbrella of SDR
[2018-03-27 21:13] http404error: Tarsier happens to dominate Hellhound but there's no rule that says it *should*
[2018-03-27 21:13] http404error: SDR is a set of rules that say what *should* dominate in Prismata
[2018-03-27 21:13] http404error: back to Lucina/Drone
[2018-03-27 21:14] http404error: here we're more clearly comparing a single attribute
[2018-03-27 21:14] http404error: but in some cases it's better to have, and in some it's not
[2018-03-27 21:14] steel0229e (Steel): @http404error about Arms race, Apooche has graphed it years ago

[2018-03-27 21:14] steel0229e (Steel): https://www.reddit.com/r/Prismata/comments/4ubv6k/strategy_question_on_how_to_play_arms_race/?st=jfa3m2jp&sh=bc9743cb
[Strategy] Question on how to play Arms Race • r/Prismata
Hello, [Arms Race for reference](https://s3.amazonaws.com/lunarch_blog/Units/Random+Set/Arms%20Race.png) I am really uncertain on how to play...
[2018-03-27 21:14] http404error: Huh, I'll have to dig into that
[2018-03-27 21:15] steel0229e (Steel): about SDR: I think it is a bad excuse that golden shield is not covered by it
[2018-03-27 21:15] steel0229e (Steel): golden shield DOES break SDR and it is self righteous to define what is and is not covered by SDR
[2018-03-27 21:15] steel0229e (Steel): by definition they are global rules that should cover everything
[2018-03-27 21:16] http404error: Believe me, I hear your argument
[2018-03-27 21:16] http404error: But I'm getting to that
[2018-03-27 21:16] Deleted User (Deleted User): I have to agree with steel hhere
[2018-03-27 21:16] http404error: Ok, so clearly golden shield *does not* dominate
[2018-03-27 21:17] http404error: to follow my previous analogy, "having golden shield" : "lacking it" :: "vivid drone" : "drone"
[2018-03-27 21:18] http404error: But like I mentioned, SDR is *not* the set of all things that ever happen to dominate in Prismata; it's the set of things that *should* by principle. Lunarch selected these principles, and they're approximated in the article.
[2018-03-27 21:18] http404error: The ones no one argues over are: more resources dominates less, more units in play dominates fewer
[2018-03-27 21:18] mrguy888: Rules are made to make the gameplay better. If breaking the rules in certain cases makes gameplay better than it should be done.
[2018-03-27 21:19] mrguy888: Rules aren't there for the sake of having rules to follow
[2018-03-27 21:19] steel0229e (Steel): I am not arguing it is bad design, I just pointed out it breaks a rule
[2018-03-27 21:19] steel0229e (Steel): and I wonder if there are other exceptions in the game like that
[2018-03-27 21:19] http404error: I don't think it breaks a rule though
[2018-03-27 21:19] http404error: I know where you're going with that, and again I'm getting there
[2018-03-27 21:19] mqp: i don't understand steel's POV, surely not every unit modifier in the game should have a dominance relation?
[2018-03-27 21:20] http404error: I'm starting to tire of getting cut off, though, so I'd appreciate your patience briefly
[2018-03-27 21:20] mqp: are you planning to design a game where units with an earlier name alphabetically dominate later ones?
[2018-03-27 21:20] http404error: I don't think that's what Steel is arguing
[2018-03-27 21:20] mqp: what about where green dominates red which dominates blue?
[2018-03-27 21:20] steel0229e (Steel): what kind of strawman is that lol
[2018-03-27 21:20] http404error: I think I can help clear this up a bit
[2018-03-27 21:20] steel0229e (Steel): you are clearly trolling
[2018-03-27 21:21] steel0229e (Steel): let http elaborate pls
[2018-03-27 21:21] http404error: Misunderstanding you is not the same thing as trolling, Steel
[2018-03-27 21:21] mqp: i didn't think it was a strawman, i thought those were examples of unit modifiers which i think should not have a dominance relation
[2018-03-27 21:21] http404error: I can sympathize with all of you, I promise
[2018-03-27 21:22] http404error: Okay, more resources = better, more units = better, more damage = better which means you should always want to kill your opponent's units, etc
[2018-03-27 21:22] http404error: Here's the tricky one that leads to this debate
[2018-03-27 21:22] http404error: ```Units with improved statuses should be better than units with worse ones```
[2018-03-27 21:23] http404error: What's an "improved status"? That's a pretty vague term. You might be able to take a few good guesses but hold on for a second there.
[2018-03-27 21:24] http404error: Unit keywords are not always "improved", naturally - many are actually strictly bad, like Frontline, Fragile, etc
[2018-03-27 21:24] http404error: Some are strictly good, like Blocker
[2018-03-27 21:24] steel0229e (Steel): it does not break this rule http
[2018-03-27 21:24] steel0229e (Steel): it breaks the "owning more units should be better than owning less"
[2018-03-27 21:25] http404error: I'm not sure I follow
[2018-03-27 21:25] steel0229e (Steel): no, I think when a golden armor tia loses you the game it breaks the rule
[2018-03-27 21:26] http404error: Can you explain how a board state where you have a golden Tia is worse than one where you have no Tia?
[2018-03-27 21:26] steel0229e (Steel): while non golden armor wincer + 5 drones can win you the game
[2018-03-27 21:26] mrguy888: Non frontline is an "improved status" over frontline. More or infinite lifespan is an "improved status" over lifespan.
[2018-03-27 21:27] http404error: @mrguy888 I agree, those happen to be true.
[2018-03-27 21:27] http404error: I believe that's intended to fall under that rule
[2018-03-27 21:27] mrguy888: Golden armor "should" be strictly better or worse to be compliant, it isn't, and that is okay.
[2018-03-27 21:27] http404error: I think the most proper way to consider the "improved status" rule is to break it down so that it explicitly lists each case
[2018-03-27 21:27] http404error: Why do you think it "should" be an improved status?
[2018-03-27 21:28] http404error: You're forcing me to jump ahead on my argument but I don't mind

--- Thread (50 messages, 2018-03-27) ---
[2018-03-27 21:28] http404error: I do want to let @Steel explain what they mean though, because I really don't understand that position at all
[2018-03-27 21:28] mrguy888: I mean it would have to be made to be strictly good or strictly bad to be compliant to SDR.
[2018-03-27 21:29] mrguy888: They even tried to do that.
[2018-03-27 21:29] mrguy888: But it turned out worse than not.
[2018-03-27 21:29] steel0229e (Steel): I was trying to say what mrguy but my explanation was a lot worse
[2018-03-27 21:30] http404error: So you think all unit keywords (including their numeric values) should be either strictly good or strictly bad?
[2018-03-27 21:30] http404error: What about non-keyworded abilities, like "start of turn" or "click" abilities?
[2018-03-27 21:31] mrguy888: Obviously not since I think golden armor is good.
[2018-03-27 21:31] steel0229e (Steel): no, I don't think SDR are normative, I just think they should be general principles
[2018-03-27 21:31] http404error: I don't know what you mean by normative.
[2018-03-27 21:31] steel0229e (Steel): "should" is normative
[2018-03-27 21:31] mrguy888: But there needs to be a very good reason for them not to be.
[2018-03-27 21:31] http404error: Right, so you think that the "improved statuses" rule is saying that all "statuses" or "keywords" should be either strictly good or strictly bad?
[2018-03-27 21:32] mrguy888: That is the spirit behind it.
[2018-03-27 21:32] http404error: Well... I don't have a definitive way to disprove that. I just think it's unlikely that that is the intention.
[2018-03-27 21:32] steel0229e (Steel): the costs of units are largely balanced around this assumption
[2018-03-27 21:33] steel0229e (Steel): that some statuses are clearly worse or better than others
[2018-03-27 21:33] http404error: That doesn't mean the game can't ever have a tradeoff status in it though
[2018-03-27 21:33] http404error: I mean, if you consider various combinations of statuses, abilities, and costs, you can come up with all kinds of tradeoffs
[2018-03-27 21:33] http404error: and things that become hard to balance
[2018-03-27 21:34] http404error: If you're talking about rebalancing a unit to add or remove an ability in a vacuum, that's kind of the case I addressed earlier that I feel isn't that important
[2018-03-27 21:34] http404error: I do have further to go on my argument though, just so you know
[2018-03-27 21:34] ponnari: calling something "not SDR compliant" isn't saying that it should be in the game, but that you should give thought wether you're okay with it breaking these guidelines
[2018-03-27 21:34] mrguy888: What do you mean by tradeoff status?
[2018-03-27 21:35] http404error: @mrguy888 I mean that hypothetically "adding fragile and also adding blocker" is a tradeoff, it's neither strictly good nor strictly bad
[2018-03-27 21:35] mrguy888: That is irrelevant to SDR
[2018-03-27 21:36] http404error: That doesn't mean it's irrelevant to the discussion at all.
[2018-03-27 21:36] mrguy888: All SDR cares about is that adding blocker is strictly good and adding fragile is strictly bad.
[2018-03-27 21:36] http404error: I don't even know why you said that.
[2018-03-27 21:36] http404error: Why does SDR care about that?
[2018-03-27 21:36] http404error: I can go more into the "balance change" model later if you'd like
[2018-03-27 21:36] mrguy888: Cause improved statuses are supposed to be better
[2018-03-27 21:37] http404error: Why does that even matter though? You can't add or remove Blocker or Fragile from a unit, so it never will come up
[2018-03-27 21:37] mqp: i guess i sympathize with mrguy's point of view, the original justification that sold me on having dominance relations for all these things is that it makes it easier to do local reasoning on positions -- that applies equally well to any kind of modifier that you're considering to buy or not buy, so i guess i moderately favor having dominance relations for all of them where it's convenient
[2018-03-27 21:37] mqp: the thing is though that for practical gameplay reasons i almost prefer having a dominance relation that is true only 98% of the time though, because it seems really satisfying to have games where you are required to notice the other 2%
[2018-03-27 21:37] mqp: so i'm very fond of e.g. golden armor's situation here
[2018-03-27 21:39] mqp: in conclusion, i really would make the game so that units earlier in the alphabet were better, only i would make them just 98% of the time better
[2018-03-27 21:39] http404error: That's a totally reasonable position to hold
[2018-03-27 21:41] http404error: Anyways, it's useful to consider game actions like "buying a unit" to be a mix of strictly good and strictly bad things
[2018-03-27 21:41] http404error: (Obviously game actions that themselves are overall one or the other are boring because they aren't real decisions)

[2018-03-27 22:40] http404error: ```Consider a situation where your opponent is attacking for 1 and you have a single Drone, an invulnerable Lucina, and no other units. If your Drone is invulnerable, the opponent will be able to take out Lucina. However, if the Drone is exhausted instead, the opponent will have to kill it before being able to attack the invulnerable Lucina (due to Prismata’s overkill rule).```
[2018-03-27 22:41] http404error: Yeah it's a totally stupid edge case and not really that important at all to the game's design
[2018-03-27 22:41] http404error: Much like anything that causes SDR arguments
[2018-03-27 22:41] mrguy888: I define a component as the smallest thing you can breat it down to.
[2018-03-27 22:41] mrguy888: You said yourself that you can't break down golden armor.
[2018-03-27 22:41] http404error: Breaking something down doesn't have any inherent meaning in the game rules though
[2018-03-27 22:41] mrguy888: You can't break down frontine.
[2018-03-27 22:42] mrguy888: You can't break down blocker.
[2018-03-27 22:42] http404error: You can if you want to consider the various rules applying to it separately
[2018-03-27 22:42] http404error: Consider the effect of hypothetical rules changes, much less unit balance changes
[2018-03-27 22:43] mrguy888: Was that your answer? I don't understand what you just said.
[2018-03-27 22:43] http404error: Golden armor is affected by both damage allocation and targeting rules.
[2018-03-27 22:44] http404error: Frontline is affected by turn phase and damage rules.
[2018-03-27 22:44] http404error: I mean, at this point the whole argument loses meaningful grip.
[2018-03-27 22:44] http404error: You could do anything with the game rules, with the meaning of keywords, with individual unit designs
[2018-03-27 22:44] http404error: The question is what is *worth* establishing dominance over
[2018-03-27 22:44] http404error: Which is really just opinion
[2018-03-27 22:45] mrguy888: "You could design a different game where that isn't true" is your arguement against frontline not being able to be broken down in Prismata?
[2018-03-27 22:46] http404error: I was moving past that because I thought you might follow me
[2018-03-27 22:46] http404error: I'll go back to that
[2018-03-27 22:51] http404error: It's not that there isn't a way to break down Frontline, it's that there isn't a way to break it down that makes sense in Prismata. You could do all sorts of nonsensical things like "partial Frontline" (say you can only damage it up to half during your turn or something) but the determination of what makes a logical break point is rather loose. The fact that multiple units are all affected by the same "frontline rule" is itself a pretty arbitrary distinction; you can make a unit that has a unique rule affecting only it just as easily. At the very least breaking down "unit name" into sub-properties is no less arbitrary. You might not be able to break down Frontline in the rules of Prismata, but neither can you break down "unit name", or actually any property that has no way to be applied or removed by another unit.
[2018-03-27 22:53] mrguy888: Yes you are correct that there isn't a way to break it down that makes sense in Prismata. That is my point.
[2018-03-27 22:54] mrguy888: Breaking down unit name into separate points also make sense.
[2018-03-27 22:54] http404error: That doesn't necessarily mean I won't come up with a way that makes sense after I go to bed tonight though! It seems like a fun exercise.
[2018-03-27 22:54] http404error: Of course argument from exhaustive proof is pretty much the entire essence of SDR anyways so...
[2018-03-27 22:55] http404error: Okay, I think you're right

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: It's not that there isn't a way to break down Frontline, it's that there isn't a way to break it down that makes sense in Prismata. You could do all sorts of nonsensical things like "partial Frontline" (say you can only damage it up to half during your turn or something) but the determination of what makes a logical break point is rather loose. The fact that multiple units are all affected by the same "frontline rule" is itself a pretty arbitrary distinction; you can make a unit that has a unique rule affecting only it just as easily. At the very least breaking down "unit name" into sub-properties is no less arbitrary. You might not be able to break down Frontline in the rules of Prismata, but neither can you break down "unit name", or actually any property that has no way to be applied or removed by another unit.
